   <item>
      <title>Jason Kidd, Mavericks Agree To Part Ways</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285714/Jason-Kidd-Mavericks-Agree-To-Part-Ways</link>
      <description><p>Jason Kidd and the Dallas Mavericks have mutually agreed to part ways. Kidd had four seasons and approximately $40 million remaining on his contract but exits the Mavericks a few months after the franchise fired Nico Harrison as general manager. The Mavericks hired Masai Ujiri as president of basketball operations this offseason with Mike Schmitz as general manager.</p>
<p>Kidd was hired by the Mavericks ahead of the 2021-22 season and guided the team to the Western Conference Finals with a roster built around Luka Doncic. The Mavericks were out of playoffs in 2023 and then went to The Finals in 2024.</p>
<p>The franchise completely pivoted in February 2025 when Harrison made the decision to trade away Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers for Anthony Davis.</p>
<p>Ujiri is now rebuilding the roster around Cooper Flagg and will select his own head coach.</p>
<p>"Jason has had a meaningful impact on the Dallas Mavericks, both as a Hall of Fame player and as the head coach who helped lead this franchise back to the NBA Finals," said Ujiri. "We are thankful for Jason's leadership, his professionalism and his commitment to the team. In my short time here, I've developed an enormous amount of respect for what he has built. He will always be an important part of the Mavericks family." </p>
<p>The Mavericks join the Chicago Bulls, Orlando Magic and Portland Trail Blazers with current head coaching openings. </p>
<p>"As we evaluate the future of our basketball program, we believe this is the right moment for a new direction for our team," Ujiri said. "We have high expectations for this franchise and a responsibility to build a basketball organization capable of sustained championship contention. We will conduct a thorough, disciplined search for our next head coach and continue to evaluate our entire basketball operations staff to ensure we compete at the standard Mavs fans expect and deserve." </p>
<p>Kidd expressed interest in a promotion to president of basketball operations following the firing of Harrison, but Patrick Dumont informed him he wouldn't be considered for the role, <a href="https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/48820152/jason-kidd-mavericks-coach-five-seasons">according to ESPN</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 19:11:11 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285714/Jason-Kidd-Mavericks-Agree-To-Part-Ways</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kidd_Jason_dal_240606.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Magic Have Billy Donovan, Jeff Van Gundy, Sean Sweeney On Head Coach Candidate List</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285710/Magic-Have-Billy-Donovan-Jeff-Van-Gundy-Sean-Sweeney-On-Head-Coach-Candidate-List</link>
      <description><p>The Orlando Magic are beginning to advance their search for a new head coach to replace Jahmal Mosley, sources tell Jake Fischer.</p>
<p>While no clear favorite has emerged, Billy Donovan is believed to have an excellent chance of being hired by the Magic. Donovan has held conversations with Jeff Weltman and will have an in-person meeting in the coming days.</p>
<p>Jeff Van Gundy is also on the radar of the Magic as they search for a head coach with experience. Van Gundy has spent the past two seasons as an assistant coach under Tyronn Lue with the Los Angeles Clippers.</p>
<p>Sean Sweeney is another candidate who will get an interview with the Magic. Sweeney is currently the top assistant coach with the San Antonio Spurs under Mitch Johnson. Sweeney doesn't have previous head coaching experience, but he is one of the top first-time candidates on the market. Sweeney is also a candidate for the Chicago Bulls' head coaching job.</p>
<p>Tom Thibodeau has interest in returning to coaching, but he not involved in the Magic search.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 13:03:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285710/Magic-Have-Billy-Donovan-Jeff-Van-Gundy-Sean-Sweeney-On-Head-Coach-Candidate-List</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Donovan_Billy_chi_240410.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>AJ Dybantsa Not Considered Lock To Be Picked At No. 1 By Wizards</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285703/AJ-Dybantsa-Not-Considered-Lock-To-Be-Picked-At-No-1-By-Wizards</link>
      <description><p>AJ Dybantsa is not currently viewed as a lock to go first overall to the Washington Wizards, according to rival teams. Unlike some drafts, there is no true consensus top prospect in the 2026 NBA draft as arguments can be made for Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cam Boozer and Caleb Wilson. Because of the lack of clarity on how those clear top-4 prospects should be ordered, there is a greater possibility of teams trading within that tier.</p>
<p>Dybantsa overtook Darryn Peterson during their freshman seasons as the safest pick at the top of the draft.</p>
<p>Dybantsa has been strongly linked with the Utah Jazz due to Ryan Smith's close ties to the BYU program. The Jazz could offer the Wizards a trade package to move up, but that is speculative at this point. Utah could maintain their future assets and simply stay at No. 2 to draft either Dybantsa or Peterson at No. 2. Peterson is a cleaner fit with Utah's existing frontcourt of Lauri Markkanen, Jaren Jackson Jr. and Walker Kessler. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 09:39:25 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285703/AJ-Dybantsa-Not-Considered-Lock-To-Be-Picked-At-No-1-By-Wizards</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dybantsa_AJ_byu_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Teams Still Waiting On Results Of Darryn Peterson's Medicals</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285704/NBA-Teams-Still-Waiting-On-Results-Of-Darryn-Petersons-Medicals</link>
      <description><p>Darryn Peterson's medical evaluations have not yet been shared with NBA teams. Those results remain the most closely watched off-court variable ahead of the NBA Draft, as teams assess the cramping issues that hampered his freshman season at Kansas. Peterson attributed the issue to high doses of creatine.</p>
<p>Team sources tell ESPN that Peterson presented as composed and focused during interviews last week, handling pointed questions about his unusual season with maturity. </p>
<p>Peterson entered the season as the top prospect in the 2026 NBA draft class, but his injury issues eventually moved AJ Dybantsa ahead of him on a majority of boards due to the BYU wing having a less risky outlook.</p>
<p>Peterson remains the prospect viewed as having the most upside if injury concerns can be assuaged. </p>
<p>Peterson also measured well at the combine with a plus-5&frac14; wingspan.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 09:45:32 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285704/NBA-Teams-Still-Waiting-On-Results-Of-Darryn-Petersons-Medicals</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Peterson_Darryn_ku_251222.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Bucks Expected To Acquire Additional 2026 NBA Draft Picks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285706/Bucks-Expected-To-Acquire-Additional-2026-NBA-Draft-Picks</link>
      <description><p>There is an expectation from the Milwaukee Bucks that they will have multiple selections in this year's draft, rival teams tell ESPN's Jeremy Woo. The Bucks have their own pick at No. 10 where they are currently mocked by ESPN to draft Nate Ament.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/tradeable">The Bucks have three tradeable first round picks</a>, but acquiring extra selections in the June draft presumes they are part of a return on a Giannis Antetokounmpo deal.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285705/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Continues-To-Prefer-Trade-From-Bucks">Antetokounmpo continues to signal he prefers a trade from the Bucks this offseason</a>.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285705/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Continues-To-Prefer-Trade-From-Bucks">The Bucks do not control their own first round pick until 2031 due to previous trades for Jrue Holiday and Damian Lillard</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 10:32:03 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285706/Bucks-Expected-To-Acquire-Additional-2026-NBA-Draft-Picks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Horst_Jon_mil_170628.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LeBron James Has Already Received Interest From Most Rival Contenders</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285708/LeBron-James-Has-Already-Received-Interest-From-Most-Rival-Contenders</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James has yet to determine whether he will play a 24th NBA season, but he continues to receive significant interest across the league at the age of 41. </p>
<p>"I wouldn't say that's something that's been determined," said ESPN's Shams Charania when asked if James has made a decision on continuing his career or retirement. "Honestly, I think LeBron, more than in past years, probably needs even more time with his family and his inner circle [to make that decision].</p>
<p>"I do know that a lot of teams are calling him and his camp. I spoke with Rich Paul at the Chicago draft combine last week, and he told me that essentially every contender in the NBA has placed a call since the season ended. So that line is going to continue to be open.</p>
<p>"But listen, all indications throughout the season have been that LeBron is going to play another year. We'll see if that remains true. He's got to take that time. We've never seen this before at 41 years old. I think if he decided to step away, no one would hold it against him. But the expectation has been that you're probably going to get one more year out of him, and we'll see whether it's with the Lakers or somewhere else.</p>
<p>"Being in LA, having that comfort, and knowing the Lakers want him, I think all of those factors do play a part."</p>
<p>Unlike 2010 and 2014 when James was a free agent in his prime and teams lined up cap space to pursue him, the NBA's contenders do not have the required cap space to sign him outright at a number similar to the $52.6 million he made this past season with the Lakers. Because of these circumstances, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285648/LeBron-James-Staying-With-Lakers-Considered-Most-Likely-As-Market-Offers-Few-Viable-Other-Options">James' decision will likely come down to returning to the Lakers at a slightly lower number or a massive discount for another team</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 10:52:07 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285708/LeBron-James-Has-Already-Received-Interest-From-Most-Rival-Contenders</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_260419.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Giannis Antetokounmpo Continues To Prefer Trade From Bucks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285705/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Continues-To-Prefer-Trade-From-Bucks</link>
      <description><p>Giannis Antetokounmpo continues to signal to the Milwaukee Bucks that he is ready to move on from the franchise this offseason via trade. After this month's lottery, ESPN's Shams Charania reported that <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285574/Bucks-Actively-Fielding-Trade-Offers-For-Giannis-Antetokounmpo">the Bucks had begun listening to trade offers for Antetokounmpo</a> and those conversations are expected to gain momentum into June. </p>
<p>"Between now and June 23rd, those conversations are going to start picking up and escalating," said Charania on Monday's episode of The Pat McAfee Show. "I reported last week on this show that the Bucks were officially open for business on all offers on all trade calls. That has continued into this week and is only going to continue.</p>
<p>"The crux of this is that Giannis has believed for a period of months now that it's time for both sides to move on. The Bucks obviously felt last year that they had a team that could compete, and when you have Giannis on your team, you do everything you can. We've seen that with their general manager Jon Horst [make trades for] Jrue Holiday in 2020, Damian Lillard in 2023, Myles Turner last summer. They're going to make swings. They're going to go all in.</p>
<p>"But this is a team that has gone all in over and over again, and I think it has reached the point where you have to ask: how much more all-in can you go? Do you even have the pieces necessary to do so? Those conversations will continue to pick up, from ownership to management, with Giannis.</p>
<p>"One thing I did pick up last week and into this week: Giannis's stance has not changed from what I've reported over the last several months. This is a situation we'll keep an eye on over the next five to six weeks."</p>
<p>The Minnesota Timberwolves, Los Angeles Lakers, New York Knicks, Miami Heat, Boston Celtics, Cleveland Cavaliers and Golden State Warriors all had interest in Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline. The Wolves and Heat had more extensive discussions on a trade.</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo will become eligible for a four-year, $275 million extension in October.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 10:26:26 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285705/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Continues-To-Prefer-Trade-From-Bucks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250214.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Trajan Langdon Says Pistons Want To Re-Sign Jalen Duren, Tobias Harris</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285715/Trajan-Langdon-Says-Pistons-Want-To-Re-Sign-Jalen-Duren-Tobias-Harris</link>
      <description><p>Detroit Pistons general manager Trajan Langdon said Tuesday the club intends to pursue contract extensions with both All-Star center Jalen Duren and veteran forward Tobias Harris following a 60-win season that ended in a Game 7 second-round loss to the Cleveland Cavaliers.</p>
<p>"We look forward to coming together with his representation," Langdon said of Duren, 22, who is eligible to become a restricted free agent this summer. Detroit would retain the right to match any outside offer.</p>
<p>Duren earned his first All-Star selection this season after averaging nearly 20 points and more than 10 rebounds during the regular season. His production dropped to 10.2 points and 8.5 rebounds per game in the playoffs as defenses made targeted adjustments to limit his opportunities near the rim. Duren is expected to be an All-NBA selection that qualifies him for up to a 30 percent max contract.</p>
<p>"Sometimes that happens with young players in the league," Langdon said. "JD had a fantastic season. He's one of the biggest contributors to us being a No. 1 seed."</p>
<p>Duren was selected 13th overall out of Memphis in the 2022 draft by Charlotte and acquired by Detroit in a draft-night trade. He averaged more than 11 points per game across his first three seasons before his breakout this year.</p>
<p>Harris, 34, is entering unrestricted free agency after the second and final year of his contract. He averaged more than 18 points per game in the playoffs, providing a reliable scoring option alongside Cade Cunningham on an otherwise defense-first roster.</p>
<p>"We hope that we can bring him back," Langdon said. "He's somebody we want to keep in a Pistons uniform."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 22:13:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285715/Trajan-Langdon-Says-Pistons-Want-To-Re-Sign-Jalen-Duren-Tobias-Harris</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Duren_Jalen_det_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Matt Lloyd, Mike Gansey, Nick U'Ren Among Candidates For Sixers' Lead Basketball Exec Job</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285712/Matt-Lloyd-Mike-Gansey-Nick-URen-Among-Candidates-For-Sixers-Lead-Basketball-Exec-Job</link>
      <description><p>Matt Lloyd, Mike Gansey and Nick U'Ren are among the candidates for the Philadelphia 76ers lead basketball executive job to replace Daryl Morey. The search is being conducted by Bob Myers, who will oversee the executive who fills the role.</p>
<p>Lloyd, who is currently general manager of the Minnesota Timberwolves under Tim Connelly, was a top candidate for the Chicago Bulls' vacancy that went to Bryson Graham. </p>
<p>Gansey is general manager of the Cleveland Cavaliers under Koby Altman and like Lloyd is considered one of the top candidates on the market to eventually be hired as the lead decision-maker.</p>
<p>U'Ren is the lead executive for the Phoenix Mercury of the WNBA and is on Myers' list. U'Ren and Myers worked together for nine years with the Golden State Warriors.</p>
<p>While Onsi Saleh would have been a candidate, the Sixers have realized the Atlanta Hawks will not make him available.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 13:17:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285712/Matt-Lloyd-Mike-Gansey-Nick-URen-Among-Candidates-For-Sixers-Lead-Basketball-Exec-Job</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Lloyd_Matt_min_260504.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Sean Sweeney Considered Candidate For Bulls, Magic Head Coaching Jobs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285711/Sean-Sweeney-Considered-Candidate-For-Bulls-Magic-Head-Coaching-Jobs</link>
      <description><p>Sean Sweeney has emerged as a head coaching candidate for both the Chicago Bulls and Orlando Magic. Sweeney is currently the lead assistant coach for the San Antonio Spurs after previously holding the same role with the Dallas Mavericks. </p>
<p>Sweeney is credited for being the architect of a Spurs' defense anchored by the generational talent of Victor Wembanyama. </p>
<p>Sweeney was also an assistant coach under Jason Kidd with the Brooklyn Nets and Milwaukee Bucks.</p>
<p>Sweeney has had a number of head coaching interviews in past offseasons.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 13:11:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285711/Sean-Sweeney-Considered-Candidate-For-Bulls-Magic-Head-Coaching-Jobs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Sweeney_Sean_sas_260515.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Mike Brown's Film Room Confrontation With Karl-Anthony Towns Led To 'Point KAT'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285709/Mike-Browns-Film-Room-Confrontation-With-Karl-Anthony-Towns-Led-To-Point-KAT</link>
      <description><p>Mike Brown was hired by the New York Knicks after they fired Tom Thibodeau despite an Eastern Conference Finals appearance in order to better utilize the depth of the roster and also to get more out of the offense. For the Knicks to take the next step on offense, they needed to better manage the pairing of Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns.</p>
<p>A blunt film session confrontation between Brown and Towns following a Game 3 loss to the Atlanta Hawks in the first round has sparked seven consecutive playoff wins and transformed Towns into one of the most productive playmaking centers in the postseason.</p>
<p>Trailing 2-1 in the first round, Brown called out Towns directly after film showed him standing in a corner instead of executing a screen for Jalen Brunson.</p>
<p>"You want the ball? What are you doing here?" Brown told Towns, per a source.</p>
<p>The confrontation launched what the Knicks now call "Point KAT." During the current winning streak, Towns is averaging 15.7 points, 9.6 rebounds, and 8.0 assists on 60 percent shooting in just 26.3 minutes. His assist rate of 18.5 percent leads all players with at least 100 passes this postseason, more than double his regular-season mark.</p>
<p>"In a way, it felt like [Brown] saying, 'Screw it, I'm going to do things my way,'" one Knicks player told ESPN of the style change.</p>
<p>"Putting KAT in that position, knowing that we have great screeners, great cutters and a great facilitator, was something that was definitely easy to say, 'Yes, let's try it,'" Brown said.</p>
<p>The offensive shift has lifted the entire roster. Mikal Bridges, scoreless in 20 minutes in the pivotal Game 3 loss, has averaged 15.6 points on 67 percent shooting since. Brunson remains at 27.4 points per game while posting his most efficient postseason as a Knick.</p>
<p>New York's offensive rating during the seven-game streak stands at 130.5 points per 100 possessions, the best among 2026 playoff teams.</p>
<p>Towns cites Magic Johnson as one of his greatest influences and is the reason why he wears No. 32. Towns is now playing more like him.</p>
<p>"He's been a big inspiration in my life, and to be able to have this moment is great," Towns said. "Like Magic, I always talk about impact and winning, and to be able to amplify my teammates is one of the greatest honors."</p>
<p>"When you have guys that pass the ball, like Draymond Green, like [Domantas] Sabonis, like KAT, you try to utilize their strengths to help others out, make others better," Brown said earlier this week.</p>
<p>"Putting KAT in that position, knowing that we have great screeners, great cutters and a great facilitator, was something that was definitely easy to say, 'Yes, let's try it.'"</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 11:11:54 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285709/Mike-Browns-Film-Room-Confrontation-With-Karl-Anthony-Towns-Led-To-Point-KAT</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Towns_Karlanthony_nyk_260420.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Matt Riccardi Leaving Mavericks' Front Office</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285716/Matt-Riccardi-Leaving-Mavericks-Front-Office</link>
      <description><p>Matt Riccardi is leaving the Dallas Mavericks' front office as the franchise changes direction under Masai Ujiri. Riccardi served as co-general manager of the Mavericks, alongside Michael Finely, following the firing of Nico Harrison.</p>
<p>Riccardi was largely responsible for the Mavericks' deadline trade of Anthony Davis to the Washington Wizards.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/staff/Matt-Riccardi/Summary/642">Riccardi joined the Mavericks from the Brooklyn Nets in 2022</a>.</p>
<p>Finley's future with the Mavericks has yet to be determined.</p>
<p>The Mavericks parted ways with Jason Kidd as head coach on Tuesday.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 22:22:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285716/Matt-Riccardi-Leaving-Mavericks-Front-Office</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_dallasmavericks.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Toronto Tempo vs. Phoenix Mercury Live Stream: Watch WNBA Season 2026 on Fubo</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285707/Toronto-Tempo-vs-Phoenix-Mercury-Live-Stream-Watch-WNBA-Season-2026-on-Fubo</link>
      <description><p style="position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; text-align: center; z-index: 9999; margin: 0; padding: 10px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);"><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 0;" type="button" value="STREAM TEMPO VS MERCURY LIVE ON FUBO TONIGHT!" /> </a></p>
<p style="position: fixed; top: 0px; left: 0px; width: 100%; text-align: center; z-index: 9999; margin: 0px; padding-top: 10px; padding-right: 10px; padding-bottom: 10px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);"> </p>
<p>The 2026 WNBA regular season rolls onward with a captivating cross-conference matchup tonight, Tuesday, May 19, as the expansion <strong>Toronto Tempo</strong> (2-1) travel southwest to face the <strong>Phoenix Mercury</strong> (1-1) at the Footprint Center. Toronto heads to the desert riding high after a spectacular 106-96 victory over the Los Angeles Sparks on Sunday. Meanwhile, the Mercury look to build on their home momentum after handing the Chicago Sky an impressive 91-83 defeat in their last outing. Watch all the action live on Fubo.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="WATCH THE TEMPO VS MERCURY ON FUBO" /></a></p>
<p>Toronto's fast start to their inaugural franchise season has been powered by a high-octane backcourt. Guard <strong>Brittney Sykes</strong> has been dynamic, recently dropping 27 points in a game, while star sharpshooter <strong>Marina Mabrey</strong> continues to space the floor perfectly for head coach Sandy Brondello's system. Phoenix counters with an equally dangerous perimeter game spearheaded by <strong>Kahleah Copper</strong>, who is fresh off a blistering 30-point performance. With premier facilitator <strong>Alyssa Thomas</strong> guiding the offense, the Mercury possess the experience to stress any defense. Witness this blockbuster live on Fubo.</p>
<p>Tactically, this matchup will come down to interior execution and depth. The Tempo enter tonight somewhat shorthanded upfront, with key frontcourt options <strong>Temi Fagbenle</strong> (shoulder) and <strong>Isabelle Harrison</strong> (hand) both ruled out. This places a massive burden on <strong>Nyara Sabally</strong> to manage the glass against a physical Phoenix front line. Out on the perimeter, look for Kia Nurse to try to limit Copper's driving lanes to force the Mercury into tough outside looks. Expect a roaring crowd in Phoenix.    <item>
      <title>Spurs Say Victor Wembanyama Used Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP As Fuel In Game 1 Win</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285702/Spurs-Say-Victor-Wembanyama-Used-Shai-Gilgeous-Alexanders-MVP-As-Fuel-In-Game-1-Win</link>
      <description><p>Victor Wembanyama confirmed Monday that watching Shai Gilgeous-Alexander receive his second straight MVP trophy before Game 1 of the Western Conference finals felt personal, a sentiment his teammates and coach say drove his historic 41-point, 24-rebound performance in the San Antonio Spurs' 122-115 double-overtime victory over the Oklahoma City Thunder.</p>
<p>Wembanyama had publicly stated his case for the award earlier this season. When asked directly if the pregame ceremony served as motivation, he simply said yes. Wembanyama answered truthfully while avoiding giving the media a clippable moment.</p>
<p>Stephon Castle made the locker room's collective view clear.</p>
<p>"We felt like that was his trophy," Castle said. "I'm sure he felt the same way."</p>
<p>Mitch Johnson confirmed the ceremony registered with his star center while stopping short of crediting it entirely for the performance.</p>
<p>"One-hundred percent," Johnson said. "To see another competitor with the trophy that he deserves - (SGA was) voted the winner. But if you're a competitor and you see another competitor get rewarded with what you want - I don't think it had any effect on the game that didn't make him play harder than anyone else - but that's motivational."</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander received the trophy from NBA commissioner Adam Silver in front of his home crowd at Paycom Center. Wembanyama sat on the bench during the ceremony, eyes fixed on the court, before delivering one of the most commanding playoff performances in NBA history.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 07:40:05 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285702/Spurs-Say-Victor-Wembanyama-Used-Shai-Gilgeous-Alexanders-MVP-As-Fuel-In-Game-1-Win</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_240516.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Duren Unlikely To Command Max Contract, Pistons Still View Him As Core Player</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285701/Jalen-Duren-Unlikely-To-Command-Max-Contract-Pistons-Still-View-Him-As-Core-Player</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Duren's disappointing postseason showing has significantly weakened his bargaining position heading into restricted free agency this summer, with the widely held belief around the league being that he holds little to no leverage to pursue a maximum contract from the Detroit Pistons.</p>
<p>Duren posted just 10.2 points and 8.5 rebounds per game on 51.4 percent shooting in the playoffs, a sharp drop from the 19.5 points, 10.5 rebounds, and 65 percent shooting that earned him his first All-Star selection during the regular season.</p>
<p>The 22-year-old was outplayed by opposing big men in both rounds, first by Wendell Carter Jr. and then Jarrett Allen. Coach J.B. Bickerstaff benched Duren in favor of Paul Reed for the entire fourth quarter and overtime of Game 5.</p>
<p>Duren could still qualify for a five-year maximum deal worth 30 percent of the salary cap if he earns an All-NBA selection, but what was possibly a difficult decision for the Pistons is now less complicated. </p>
<p>Detroit still intends to retain Duren, though at a lower figure than previously anticipated. The Pistons view his combination of size, skill, and age as difficult to replicate elsewhere, and there is organizational optimism that the poor playoff showing will serve as motivation.</p>
<p>The upcoming offseason also includes an extension decision on Ausar Thompson, whom Detroit views as a core piece and intends to extend.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 07:34:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285701/Jalen-Duren-Unlikely-To-Command-Max-Contract-Pistons-Still-View-Him-As-Core-Player</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Duren_Jalen_det_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander After Game 1: I Have To Be Better, Especially Against Team Of This Caliber</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285700/Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-After-Game-1-I-Have-To-Be-Better-Especially-Against-Team-Of-This-Caliber</link>
      <description><p>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander was held well below his usual standard as the San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 122-115 in double overtime in Game 1 of the Western Conference finals Monday. The two-time MVP finished with 24 points and 12 assists in 51 minutes but shot just 7-of-23 from the floor with four turnovers.</p>
<p>San Antonio's defensive scheme centered on consistent double-teams of Gilgeous-Alexander while Victor Wembanyama, the unanimous Defensive Player of the Year, sagged off perimeter shooters to protect the paint.</p>
<p>"We just got to be better -- me, in particular," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "I have to be better, especially against a team of this caliber. Nothing more than that."</p>
<p>The strategy created an opening for Alex Caruso, who converted 8-of-14 three-point attempts for a playoff-career-high 31 points. Gilgeous-Alexander made just three of his first 14 field goal attempts before a late personal flurry helped Oklahoma City erase a 10-point deficit and force overtime.</p>
<p>Wembanyama blocked three shots and his rim presence visibly altered Thunder driving lanes throughout the game.</p>
<p>"It's obviously challenging -- very tall, very long, deters a lot of things at the rim," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "You've got to be smart when you go in there. Be patient, but also be aggressive."</p>
<p>Mark Daigneault expressed confidence in Oklahoma City's ability to adjust.</p>
<p>"We're going to have to figure that out," Daigneault said. "One of the things that I love about this team is our problem-solving. We've been in these series before. We've hit these types of plateaus."</p>
<p>Game 2 is scheduled for Wednesday in Oklahoma City.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 19 May 2026 07:28:33 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285700/Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-After-Game-1-I-Have-To-Be-Better-Especially-Against-Team-Of-This-Caliber</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Gilgeousalexander_Shai_okc_260501.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Victor Wembanyama Posts 41 Points, 24 Rebounds In Spurs' Game 1 Double OT Win Over Thunder</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285698/Victor-Wembanyama-Posts-41-Points-24-Rebounds-In-Spurs-Game-1-Double-OT-Win-Over-Thunder</link>
      <description><p>The San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 122-115 in double overtime in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals on Monday night.</p>
<p>Victor Wembanyama finished the game with 41 points and 24 rebounds, with Dylan Harper adding 24 points and a franchise playoff-record seven steals.</p>
<p>"A great effort -- from everybody," said Wembanyama, who became the youngest player to finish with at least 40 points and 20 rebounds in a playoff game.</p>
<p>Harper also became the first rookie since Magic Johnson to finish a playoff game with at least 20 points, 10 rebounds, five assists, and five steals.</p>
<p>"It was a war of wills," Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said. "The levels of mental toughness exuded by both teams ... we needed every second from everybody that played."</p>
<p>Alex Caruso lead the Thunder with 31 points. After receiving the MVP trophy before the game, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander finished with 24 points on 7-of-24 shooting. </p>
<p>It was Gilgeous-Alexander's worst first half in terms of shooting in nearly three years.</p>
<p>"We have to get better from this game," Thunder coach Mark Daigneault said.</p>
<p>Game 2 of the series takes place on Wednesday with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">the Thunder 6.5 point favorites as they were in Game 1</a>. However, the series odds have shifted to a virtual toss-up after San Antonio's Game 1 win.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285697/Joe-Ingles-Signs-Two-Year-Deal-With-Melbourne-United</link>
      <description><p>Joe Ingles has confirmed he will sign with Melbourne United on a two-year deal. Ingles began his career in 2006 with the South Dragons and won an NBL championship with the club.</p>
<p>After leaving Australia in 2009, Ingles spent three seasons playing in Spain for Granada and Barcelona before joining Maccabi Tel Aviv for the 2013-14 season. Ingles then left for the NBA ahead of the 2014-15 season when initially joining the Los Angeles Clippers before settling in with the Utah Jazz.</p>
<p>Ingles' tenure with the Jazz ended with a torn ACL in January 2022. Ingles went on to have stints with the Milwaukee Bucks and Orlando Magic before spending the past two seasons with the Minnesota Timberwolves. </p>
<p>"Coming home to Melbourne feels incredibly special," Ingles said.</p>
<p>"To return to where my professional career began almost 20 years ago is something I don't take for granted.</p>
<p>"So much has happened since then, but Melbourne has always felt like home.</p>
<p>"We're really excited to reconnect with the community here, and to be closer to family and friends again. After so many years overseas, that part means a lot to us."</p></description>

      <description><p>Bob Myers will reportedly explore hiring Atlanta Hawks general manager Onsi Saleh as the next president of basketball operations for the Philadelphia 76ers, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>

      <title>Donovan Mitchell Says Cavs 'Can Breathe A Little Bit' Following Game 7 Win Over Pistons</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285686/Donovan-Mitchell-Says-Cavs-Can-Breathe-A-Little-Bit-Following-Game-7-Win-Over-Pistons</link>
      <description><p>The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Detroit Pistons 125-94 in Game 7 to win the Eastern Conference semifinals on Sunday night.</p>
<p>Donovan Mitchell finished the game with 26 points, 8 assists, and 6 rebounds. Mitchell was responsible for 43 points on the night, with his teammates shooting 8-for-8 off his passes.</p>
<p>"Even last year, when we lost to Indiana, we had our goals set on getting to the Finals. We're just one step closer," Mitchell said. "But, yeah it's been almost a decade of running into the same issue. So for sure, I personally, and as a team, we can breathe a little bit."</p>
<p>Sam Merrill finished with 23 points off the bench, including five 3-pointers. Evan Mobley added 21 points and 10 rebounds, with Jarrett Allen getting 23 points and 7 rebounds.</p>
<p>"I've been saying all year we have a lot to prove," Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson said after the win. "We still have more to prove. That's the next part of it, but we proved something to ourselves, that we could take that another step."</p>
<p>The Cavs have lost in the second round of the playoffs the last two years and have not made a conference final since 2018.</p>
<p>Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals against the Knicks starts on Tuesday at Madison Square Garden. </p>
<p> </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 18 May 2026 01:55:34 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285686/Donovan-Mitchell-Says-Cavs-Can-Breathe-A-Little-Bit-Following-Game-7-Win-Over-Pistons</guid>

   </item>

      <title>Mike Brown Encouraged By OG Anunoby Progress, Still Unsure About Game 1 Status</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285685/Mike-Brown-Encouraged-By-OG-Anunoby-Progress-Still-Unsure-About-Game-1-Status</link>
      <description><p>OG Anunoby participated in two consecutive practices for the New York Knicks over the weekend as he recovers from a right hamstring injury.</p>
<p>"It didn't feel as bad as the past when it happened," Anunoby said. "So knowing that, just trying to improve it day by day."</p>
<p>With the Knicks set to begin their playoff series against the Cavs on Tuesday, coach Mike Brown was still unsure about Anunoby's status for the opener.</p>
<p>"To go through that is not fun during this time of the year, because a lot of things come into play," Brown said. "We're trying to get to the top of the mountain during this time of the year, and you know, it needs luck. You need some skill. You need everybody to be healthy to a certain degree. So all that stuff, you want, knock on wood, to be going the right way for you whenever game time comes."</p>
<p>Anunoby is averaging 21.4 points per game in the playoffs on 61.9% shooting from the field and 53.8% shooting from three.</p></description>
